name: Christian Fiction
description: Fiction written from a Christian worldview, often featuring faith, redemption, or spiritual struggle as central themes.

name: Biblical Fiction
description: Fiction set in or drawing directly from biblical events, eras, or figures.

name: Inspirational Fiction
description: Uplifting fiction centered on personal growth, hope, or faith, not necessarily doctrinal.

name: Literary Fiction
description: Character- and theme-driven fiction prioritizing prose craft and ideas over plot mechanics.

name: Historical Fiction
description: Fiction set in a specific, researched historical period, where setting shapes plot and character.

name: Mystery
description: Plot centers on solving a crime or uncovering a hidden truth, with clues and a resolution.

name: Cozy Mystery
description: Low-violence mystery with an amateur sleuth, small community setting, and light tone.

name: Psychological Thriller
description: Suspense driven by unreliable perception, mental tension, and internal threat rather than action.

name: Thriller
description: Fast-paced plot built around escalating danger, stakes, and pursuit.

name: Suspense
description: Tension sustained through withheld information and anticipation of danger, often slower-building than thriller.

name: Crime Fiction
description: Fiction centered on criminal acts, investigators, or the criminal underworld.

name: Legal Thriller
description: Thriller built around courtroom proceedings, lawyers, or the justice system.

name: Domestic Suspense
description: Suspense arising from family, marriage, or household relationships rather than external threats.

name: Espionage
description: Fiction centered on spies, intelligence operations, and covert conflict.

name: Techno-thriller
description: Thriller driven by technology, military hardware, or scientific systems as a central plot engine.

name: Science Fiction
description: Speculative fiction grounded in scientific or technological premises.

name: Fantasy
description: Speculative fiction featuring magic or invented worlds not bound by real-world physical law.

name: Urban Fantasy
description: Fantasy set in a contemporary, real-world urban setting with hidden magical elements.

name: Epic Fantasy
description: Large-scale fantasy with expansive world-building, often multi-volume, high stakes.

name: Magical Realism
description: Fiction grounded in a realistic setting where one or a few magical elements are treated as unremarkable.

name: Metaphysical Fiction
description: Fiction exploring the nature of reality, consciousness, or existence, often blending philosophy and narrative.

name: Paranormal Romance
description: Romance plot combined with supernatural or paranormal elements (vampires, shifters, ghosts, etc.).

name: Horror
description: Fiction designed to frighten, unsettle, or evoke dread through supernatural or psychological threat.

name: Gothic Fiction
description: Atmospheric fiction blending horror, romance, and decay, often with a brooding setting.

name: Romance
description: Central plot is the development of a romantic relationship, typically with an optimistic ending.

name: Clean & Wholesome Romance
description: Romance without explicit sexual content or profanity.

name: Women's Fiction
description: Fiction centered on a woman's personal, relational, or emotional journey, broader than romance alone.

name: Family Saga
description: Multi-generational fiction tracing a family's history, relationships, and conflicts over time.

name: Coming of Age
description: Fiction centered on a young protagonist's transition to maturity or self-understanding.

name: Young Adult
description: Fiction written for and marketed to teen readers, typically featuring a teen protagonist.

name: Middle Grade
description: Fiction for readers roughly ages 8-12, age-appropriate themes and shorter length.

name: Dystopian
description: Fiction set in a bleak, oppressive, or societally collapsed future or alternate world.

name: Post-Apocalyptic
description: Fiction set after a civilization-ending catastrophic event.

name: War Fiction
description: Fiction centered on wartime conflict, combat, or its effects on soldiers and civilians.

name: Adventure
description: Plot driven by physical journey, exploration, or external quest and peril.

name: Western
description: Fiction set in the American frontier era, typically featuring rugged, lawless settings.

name: Satire
description: Fiction using irony, exaggeration, or ridicule to critique society, institutions, or human folly.

name: Humor
description: Fiction whose primary aim is to entertain through comedy.

name: Southern Fiction
description: Fiction steeped in the culture, dialect, and setting of the American South.

name: Multicultural Fiction
description: Fiction centered on the experience of a specific cultural or ethnic community.

name: Short Story Collection
description: A collected volume of standalone short fiction, often thematically linked.

name: Classic Literature
description: Fiction in the tradition of canonical literary works, often literary and structurally ambitious.

name: New Adult
description: Fiction featuring protagonists in their late teens to twenties, navigating early adulthood.

name: Christian Historical Fiction
description: Historical fiction written from a Christian worldview, set in a researched historical period.

name: Christian Mystery & Suspense
description: Mystery or suspense fiction written from a Christian worldview, typically without graphic content.
